title: Portable WebGPU softmax and quantized matmul study
summary: Studied current WebGPU softmax, MatMulNBits, DP4A, attention routing, and recent upstream changes. Windows subgroup matrices are excluded; portable DP4A, wide-tile, and generic paths are relevant.
findings: Online max/sum softmax replaced two-pass reduction and should be tested for long-context parity and throughput.
MatMulNBits routes among portable DP4A, wide-tile, and generic kernels when subgroup matrices are unavailable.
Vendor-sensitive route selection requires NVIDIA, AMD, and Intel measurements.

task_proposals.title: Evaluate online softmax for long-context Gemma 4 and Qwen 3.5
task_proposals.hypothesis: ORT's online softmax state merge can improve long-context throughput without logit drift.

task_proposals.title: Audit portable MatMulNBits routing without subgroup matrices
task_proposals.hypothesis: Forced DP4A, wide-tile, and generic routes will identify a conformant per-device winner.

title: Qwen 3.5 recurrent state and Gemma 4 semantics study
summary: Studied Qwen 3.5 hybrid recurrent state, Gemma 4 per-layer conditioning, WebGPU graph capture, position IDs, and known WebGPU test limitations.
findings: Qwen 3.5 recurrent-state infrastructure is suitable as a differential semantic reference.
Gemma 4 parity must cover per-layer conditioning and shared-KV inputs, not only final logits.
UpdatePositionIds in the WebGPU interface is relevant to captured decode replay correctness.
Batch size greater than one remains skipped for WebGPU in upstream C API tests.

task_proposals.title: Build Qwen 3.5 recurrent-state differential harness against ORT GenAI
task_proposals.hypothesis: Per-step recurrent tensors will localize the first AMD divergence for Qwen 3.5 2B/4B.

task_proposals.title: Validate Gemma 4 conditioning and shared-KV semantics against ORT GenAI
task_proposals.hypothesis: Named intermediate parity will identify incorrect Gemma 4 per-layer inputs.

task_proposals.title: Validate dynamic position IDs under captured decode replay
task_proposals.hypothesis: Dynamic position IDs must match uncaptured decode before replay performance is measured.

title: Portable Vulkan decode techniques study
summary: Studied the pinned release through detailed local Vulkan, decode, and KV-cache analyses. Cooperative matrices are excluded; portable DP4A, fusion, batching, and submission techniques remain applicable.
findings: Portable Q8 DP4A and vectorized dequant are relevant decode paths.
Fused RMSNorm-mul and matmul-bias can reduce dispatch overhead.
Batched prefill and adaptive submission should be tuned per device.

task_proposals.title: Benchmark and tune portable DP4A Q8 decode against llama.cpp
task_proposals.hypothesis: Portable DP4A can close the Q8 decode gap after conformance passes.

task_proposals.title: Evaluate fused RMSNorm-mul and matmul-bias dispatch reduction
task_proposals.hypothesis: Portable fusion will reduce dispatch count without logit drift.

task_proposals.title: Tune batched prefill and adaptive submission by device
task_proposals.hypothesis: Device-specific batching can improve prompt throughput while retaining portable fallbacks.

title: Conformance-first optimization guidance study
summary: Studied model architecture, fast decode, KV-cache, llama.cpp comparison, and general optimization guidance.
findings: Portable levers include residency, reuse, command replay, DP4A, f16, tiling, and fusion.
Qwen recurrent state must remain excluded from unsafe generic warmup and capture.

task_proposals.title: Create layerwise cross-device parity bisector
task_proposals.hypothesis: Automated tensor comparison can report the first divergent operation on AMD and Intel.

task_proposals.title: Profile GPU residency, allocations, and dispatch gaps
task_proposals.hypothesis: Revision-linked timelines will identify the highest-value portable bottlenecks.

title: Ragged attention, paged KV cache, and kernel autotuning study
summary: Studied the versioned Modular source under D:/workspace/project/modular, focusing on its MAX inference implementation. The most portable ideas for Backpack are layer-level differential harnesses, ragged prefill/decode shape coverage with paged KV state, and reproducible parameter-grid autotuning. CUDA graph and hardware-specific matrix paths are not transferred to Windows WebGPU.
findings: The shared ragged-attention harness varies batch size, new-token sequence length, and existing context length while allocating and releasing paged KV-cache state per request.

The Gemma 4 harness models local versus global attention, separate head dimensions, per-head Q/K normalization, sliding windows, and the attention K-equals-V case, but explicitly has no correctness reference; Backpack must add differential evidence before using its performance structure.

The Qwen 3 harness compares layer output with a reference implementation for prefill batch size one, illustrating a useful layer-level correctness pattern, but it is not evidence for Qwen 3.5 recurrent-state correctness.
The flash-attention design uses tiled K/V traversal and online softmax to avoid materializing the score matrix; only portable WebGPU variants should be considered.
kbench separates parameter-grid definition, compilation, execution, and structured result output, a useful model for revision-linked per-device WGSL tuning.

task_proposals.title: Add Gemma 4 local/global attention differential harness
task_proposals.hypothesis: Layer-level comparison across local/global attention, Q/K normalization, sliding windows, and K-equals-V will localize Gemma 4 conformance failures before performance work.

task_proposals.title: Exercise ragged prefill and paged KV decode shape matrix
task_proposals.hypothesis: A batch/sequence/context-length matrix with explicit KV page lifecycle will expose decode-state and boundary errors across NVIDIA, AMD, and Intel before recording throughput.

task_proposals.title: Create revision-linked portable WGSL autotuning grids
task_proposals.hypothesis: Reproducible per-device grids for tile size, workgroup size, vector width, and dispatch batching can select portable winners after conformance passes.
